title: buildah ftbfs on 32bit
body:
**Description**
buildah ftbfs on 32bit arches.  this is causing podman to failed to build for the 32bit arches on the ubuntu ppa.

```
# github.com/containers/buildah
./add.go:300:8: cannot use atime.Unix() (type int64) as type int32 in field value
./add.go:300:27: cannot use atime.UnixNano() % 1000000000 (type int64) as type int32 in field value
./add.go:301:8: cannot use mtime.Unix() (type int64) as type int32 in field value
./add.go:301:27: cannot use mtime.UnixNano() % 1000000000 (type int64) as type int32 in field value
Makefile:34: recipe for target 'binary' failed
```
The problem area in the code is here
```
mtime := info.ModTime()
atime := mtime
times := []syscall.Timespec{
    {Sec: atime.Unix(), Nsec: atime.UnixNano() % 1000000000},
    {Sec: mtime.Unix(), Nsec: mtime.UnixNano() % 1000000000},
}
```
It looks like on 32bit systems, a syscall.timespec expects an int32 but .Unix() returns a int64 and therefore the type mismatch.  In the golang docs for syscall, they apparently prefer the use of x/sys/unix instead which happens to have a TimeToTimeSpec which handles the bitness issue (https://godoc.org/golang.org/x/sys/unix#Timespec)

However, when making this change, it required the change of some function input types and eventually spread throughout the code.

This could also be handled by making a func that returns the []syscall.TimeSpec from a _64 and _32 file as well.

I'm fine to do a PR for this, but would like to get some direction first. Should I:

* convert syscall.timespecs to unix.timespecs and follow through on where-ever that might lead me
* just make _32 and  _64 files and move on
* claim buildah should not support 32bit arches and therefore neither should podman?

comments.body: Does using a formulation like `syscall.NsecToTimespec(atime.UnixNano())` instead of doing the math ourselves work better?
